owner: Claude Code West — West Worksite Supervisor
purpose: Define the canonical three-tier command approval model for AI_OS workers. Reduces repetitive low-risk read-only approval friction while preserving all protected-mutation and governance gates. This file is the machine-readable authority bridging AGENTS.md behavioral rules and settings.local.json enforcement.

approval_recommendation_defaults.DEFAULT_PROCEED_MEANING: continue next safe governed DRY_RUN/non-destructive step

authority_references: AGENTS.md — AI_OS Approval Friction Reduction Standard

TIER_0_AUTO.label: SAFE_READ_ONLY
TIER_0_AUTO.policy: Proceed automatically without prompting the operator.
conditions: Command is strictly read-only — no writes, no mutations, no side effects.
Command path is scoped to C:\Dev\Ai.Os\ or GitHub CLI state.
An active AI_OS EXECUTION TOKEN packet is present OR command is a plain inspection.
TIER_0_AUTO.notes: TIER_0 commands may be grouped and executed in sequence without per-command operator approval. Worker must still report what was read. Worker must still stop before any mutation.

TIER_1_LOW_RISK.label: SCOPED_APPROVED_MUTATION
TIER_1_LOW_RISK.policy: Ask once per packet context. Proceed after single operator approval. Do not ask again for the same action class within the same approved lane.
conditions: An active AI_OS EXECUTION TOKEN packet is in scope.
The command target is within the packet's allowed_paths.
The command is bounded, scoped, and reversible.
Operator has not overridden to TIER_2 for this session.
TIER_1_LOW_RISK.notes: TIER_1 auto-proceed expires at packet boundary. A new packet requires fresh approval. git commit requires safe-commit gate PASS before TIER_1 applies. git add must name exact files — never broad staging.

TIER_2_HUMAN_REQUIRED.label: HARD_STOP_MUTATION
TIER_2_HUMAN_REQUIRED.policy: Stop. Report. Wait for explicit Human Owner instruction in the active session. No auto-proceed path exists.
TIER_2_HUMAN_REQUIRED.conditions: Any one of the following triggers TIER_2 regardless of tier classification.
TIER_2_HUMAN_REQUIRED.notes: Human Owner instruction overrides TIER_2 for the specific action named, in the current session only. This override does not persist across sessions.

scope_guards.description: These guards override tier classification and force TIER_2 regardless of command type. They are non-overridable.

guards.reason: Credential and key paths are always human-gated.
Trading and broker execution paths are permanently blocked.
Root authority and canonical governance files require Human Owner approval.
Validator and policy mutation requires Human Owner approval.
Live runtime and operator automation paths require Human Owner approval.
Broad staging is never permitted in AI_OS workflows.
Commands targeting paths outside the active repo require explicit Human Owner authorization.

rollback_protections.name: Tier Override is Packet-Scoped Only
rollback_protections.rule: A TIER_0 classification can only be demoted to TIER_1 or TIER_2 — never promoted. Promotion of TIER_2 to any lower tier is not permitted under any condition.

rollback_protections.name: Scope Guards are Non-Overridable
rollback_protections.rule: All seven scope guards cannot be waived by packet configuration, AGENTS.md update, or settings.local.json entry. They are hardcoded constraints in this policy.

rollback_protections.name: Allow List Must Not Permit Mutations
rollback_protections.rule: No settings.local.json allow-list entry may permit git reset, git clean, git push --force, Remove-Item, file writes, file deletes, or credential-path reads. A proposed allow entry matching any TIER_2 command pattern must be rejected.

rollback_protections.name: TIER_1 Requires Active Packet Context
rollback_protections.rule: TIER_1 auto-proceed applies only when an active AI_OS EXECUTION TOKEN packet is in scope. Without an active packet, all mutation commands fall back to TIER_2 regardless of tier classification.

rollback_protections.name: Validator Chain Cannot Be Bypassed by Tier
rollback_protections.rule: Tier classification does not substitute for the validator chain. VALIDATOR_CHAIN_001 must run before every APPLY regardless of which commands were auto-approved during the session.

rollback_protections.name: Human Owner Override is Session-Scoped
rollback_protections.rule: If the Human Owner declares 'ask me before every git command' in a session, all git commands revert to TIER_2 for that session. Session-level operator overrides take precedence over tier classification.

rollback_protections.name: Policy File Writes are TIER_2
rollback_protections.rule: This policy file itself is in a protected path (automation/orchestration/policy/). Writes to this file require Human Owner APPLY authorization. Workers may not self-modify their own tier policy.

rollback_protections.name: settings.local.json Changes Require Human Owner Authorization
rollback_protections.rule: The settings.local.json allow list is a protected governance file. Changes require an approved APPLY packet. Workers cannot self-authorize changes to their own permission list.

fields.session_packet_id: Active packet ID from AI_OS EXECUTION TOKEN

audit_schema.retention: Retain for lifetime of packet plus 90 days. Do not delete without Human Owner authorization.

settings_local_json_reference.purpose: The settings.local.json allow list at ~/.claude/settings.local.json is the Claude Code machine-level enforcement layer. This policy file is the canonical human-readable authority. The allow list entries should reflect TIER_0 command patterns from this policy with repo-scoped path anchors.
settings_local_json_reference.note: settings.local.json is not tracked by git. This policy file provides the auditable record of intended allow-list coverage.
